alternative_parents.description: Organic salt compounds containing a transition metal atom in its ionic form.
alternative_parents.name: Organic transition metal salts

alternative_parents.description: Organic salt compounds containing a metalloid atom in its ionic form.
alternative_parents.name: Organic metalloid salts

alternative_parents.description: Aliphatic Hydrocarbons that contains one or more unsaturated carbon atoms. These compounds contain one or more double or triple bonds.
alternative_parents.name: Unsaturated aliphatic hydrocarbons

alternative_parents.description: Derivatives of hydrocarbons obtained by substituting one or more carbon atoms by an heteroatom. They contain at least one carbon atom and heteroatom.
alternative_parents.name: Hydrocarbon derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds that have a negative electric charge.
alternative_parents.name: Organic anions

class.description: Hydrocarbons that contains one or more unsaturated carbon atoms. These compounds contain one or more double, triple, or aromatic bonds.
class.name: Unsaturated hydrocarbons

description: This compound belongs to the class of organic compounds known as cycloalkenes. These are unsaturated monocyclic hydrocarbons having one endocyclic double bond.

direct_parent.description: Unsaturated monocyclic hydrocarbons having one endocyclic double bond.
direct_parent.name: Cycloalkenes

intermediate_nodes.description: Olefins that contain at least one ring in their structure.
intermediate_nodes.name: Cyclic olefins

kingdom.description: Compounds that contain at least one carbon atom, excluding isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, Carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
kingdom.name: Organic compounds

subclass.description: Acyclic and cyclic hydrocarbons having one or more carbon-carbon double bonds, apart from the formal ones in aromatic compounds. The class of olefins subsumes alkenes and cycloalkenes and the corresponding polyenes.
subclass.name: Olefins

superclass.description: Organic compounds made up only of carbon and hydrogen atoms.
superclass.name: Hydrocarbons
